note: The official 2025-2026 Handbook places two selectable Majors under the current PERM Programme: the established Physical Education and Recreation Management (PERM) Major and the new Sports Science Major available by application to students admitted in 2025/26. They are modelled as separate local Majors so course state remains isolated by Programme, Major and Course. The Social Sciences Common Core applies to both. For PERM, the page publishes 40 units of Major Required Courses and a base 17-unit Major Elective pattern: five theory electives (15 units) plus two skills electives (2 units). Because both required PERM internships fulfil the 3-unit Experiential Learning in Action component, the official cross-credit rule requires 3 additional units of PERM Major Elective course(s), making 20 elective units in total. For Sports Science, the page publishes 45 units of Major Required Courses and 15 units selected from its published elective list. Its required pathway uses both CMED1046 and CMED3005, or, for students not proficient in Chinese, both PERM3066 and PERM3067. PERM4898 and PERM4899 together form the 6-unit Honours Project for either Major. Three codes have minor title differences between the two programme-table sections; PERM1316, PERM3027 and PERM1435 use the titles on their linked 2025-2026 Handbook course pages. The published elective options are retained without inferring any unstated availability or automatic completion rule. University Language Courses, General Education Courses and Free Electives are excluded. No Year or Term is assigned.
